steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: ”Walking decisions are made in complex contexts in which multiple conditions and needs inform individuals' choices.” This sentence explains why copying one feature from Tokyo—like its population density—cannot be a reliable strategy for increasing walkability elsewhere.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
The passage argues that identifying a reliable way to increase walking is difficult precisely because multiple factors, not a single feature, drive behavior.

Answer C states this main purpose directly.
  • A narrows to density alone (which the passage says is insufficient)
  • B frames the issue as actionable improvement (the text only identifies a problem)
  • D confuses the issue with comparing traffic levels rather than increasing them
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
